title: The Chronicles of Carland and the Sea Mice
summary: The saga begins in the crushing depths of the ocean, where the explorer Sansango Cantasa reaches the seabed to find the elusive Sea Cat. In the world above the floor, a vast community of seamice—featured in a library of specialized books—contemplates building a grand ocean castle while avoiding deep-sea sharks. As the mysterious presence of Carland draws near, the narrative shifts into a world of artistic creation. Creatures of all kinds, from butterfly-hedgehogs to silver-star owls and dinosaurs that look like snowflakes, emerge as vibrant paper figures. In this whimsical realm, a seamouse might find itself gifted with a tiny hat, while others navigate the festivities behind the guise of a fox mask.

highlightQuote.text: One seamouse, two seamouses, three seamouses, four seamouses, ten for Sinong, ten seamouses, twelve seamouses, ten to thirteen seamouses.

transcript.text: There's plenty of paper. We can make things out of paper, Dada.

transcript.text: That's true. What do you want to make?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you want to make animal... Dad, do you want to make animals out of paper?

transcript.text: I would love to make animals out of paper with you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Would you like to do it? Let's do... let's use some pink first. Let's use pink for first stars. And we use red for next.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's see. One way to do this would be to rip out little shapes and make the bear by attaching them back together with some glue. Another way would be to cut out...

Here's one strip and then I'll make another one that's about the same length and thickness by tearing it like that. That made an X. Then if I make another one I can make kind of a star. Look at that. And now if I attach all those together with a sticker. Which one should I use? Maybe I'll use an owl for my star.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This could be an animal. It's a dinosaur.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then we do red after. Oh, it's almost more like a snowflake actually. I like that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dada, red.

transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now look what I did here. I made a long strip and I curled it around and now if I tape the ends together with this fox sticker... then it can stand up. Look at that. I made a little tube. That could be a hat actually.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi mouse, we made a hat for you!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh wow, says Happy. Would anybody like to wear it? Would you like to wear it? Maybe you could try it on. I think that might fit you. Oh, it's a little too small for this person. Maybe a mouse would like it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait no, maybe it would fit on her.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's see. Oh, it doesn't fit because of her pigtails.

transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh, it's a fox mask! That's so funny. She's dressed as a fox now.

characters.name: Sansango Cantasa
characters.description: An intrepid deep-sea explorer who dives into the high-pressure depths.

characters.name: Sea Cat
characters.description: A mysterious feline inhabitant living at the very bottom of the ocean floor.

characters.name: Zanna
characters.description: A character who sets out to find a bouncy mence.

characters.name: Seamouse
characters.description: A nautical rodent and the subject of several books, occasionally seen wearing a hat.

characters.name: Carland
characters.description: A mysterious figure from a book who is said to be coming and is known to be smooth.

characters.name: Honna Fisibizle
characters.description: A rhythmic character mentioned in tales of the sea mice.

characters.name: Miceize the Jowse
characters.description: A peculiar mouse featured in the expanding stories of Carland.

characters.name: Zonner Zenzy
characters.description: A character dwelling within the strange narratives of the deep.

characters.name: Bonner Sanassol
characters.description: A name associated with the escaping mice and their adventures.

characters.name: Butterfly Hedgehog
characters.description: A hybrid paper creature adorned with sparkles.

characters.name: Fox
characters.description: Behind a ceremonial mask and a playful sticker, this solitary navigator charts both the stars and the thicket, serving as a forest inhabitant who provides directions in exchange for a breakfast of flowers and juice. A steadfast ritualist and fire-extinguisher, they toll the iconic bell for the nibbler while finding more solace in space tacos than the decadent elixirs brewed in their honor. Whether navigating the heavy void or the woodland path, they remain a wise neighbor of cosmic perspective, pointedly advising seekers that the path to clarity begins with a simple bath. Bound to the chime that draws the visitor, they are a figure of quiet contrast—a guide who understands that the weight of destiny is best balanced by the essential purities of the everyday.

characters.name: Dinosaur
characters.description: A paper-crafted animal that resembles a red snowflake.

places.name: Deep Water
places.description: A high-pressure zone far beneath the surface where explorers roam.

places.name: Bottom of the Floor
places.description: The deepest part of the ocean, home to the Sea Cat.

places.name: Ocean Castle
places.description: A grand underwater structure built by the seafaring mice.

places.name: Carland
places.description: A legendary land or entity that is steadily approaching.
